name: notan
description: Portable, modular multimedia/game framework with strong web support

kind.comment: Self-described as a portable multimedia layer — "not an engine". A code-first framework: window/graphics/input/audio with a state + update/draw loop and no editor.

license.comment: Dual-licensed MIT OR Apache-2.0 per crates.io version metadata (GitHub's single detected license shows Apache-2.0).

documentation.comment: API docs on docs.rs; runnable examples are hosted at nazariglez.github.io/notan-web. No dedicated book/guide.

first-release.comment: First crates.io publish in February 2022; the GitHub repo dates back to 2019-10 (earlier iterations predate the crates.io crate).

latest-stable-version.comment: Newest stable on crates.io as of 2026-07-06 (released 2026-01-17).

maintenance-status.comment: Described by the author as in slow maintenance mode while focusing on other projects; roughly yearly releases (0.13 in 2025-03, 0.14 in 2026-01) and last push 2026-01-17.

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 (0.14.x) after ~4 years on crates.io; usable and reasonably stable for 2D apps/demos but still evolving with occasional breaking changes between minor series.

backing.comment: No company or foundation backing; effectively a single-maintainer project.

implementation.comment: Pure Rust; renders through glow (OpenGL/WebGL) with only platform glue — no engine *-sys bindings.

architecture.comment: Code-first: a user State plus update/draw callbacks each frame, with a modular plugin/graphics-extension system. No scene graph or ECS.

built-in-ecs.comment: No ECS; game state is plain Rust structs held in the app State.

dimensionality.comment: 2D focus (draw2d API). The low-level graphics API lets you issue custom GL draw calls/shaders for 3D by hand, but there is no first-party 3D tooling (camera, meshes, lighting).

scripting.comment: Gameplay is written in Rust; no embedded scripting language.

data-driven-scenes.comment: No scene/prefab file format; everything is constructed in code.

reflection-serialization.comment: No first-party runtime reflection or scene serialization; users bring serde themselves.

hot-reload.comment: No built-in code or asset hot-reloading in the framework.

visual-editor.comment: Code-first framework; ships no graphical scene/level editor.

live-inspector.comment: No runtime world/entity inspector; the built-in egui integration can be used to hand-roll debug panels.

asset-pipeline.comment: Async asset loading of common formats (textures, audio, etc.) via an AssetList/asset-loading API; no import/processing pipeline or reprocessing.

cli-project-tooling.comment: No dedicated project-scaffolding CLI or cargo plugin; you add notan as a normal dependency.

debug-profiling-tools.comment: No first-party profiler/diagnostics integrations; the egui plugin can be used to build ad-hoc debug overlays.

ide-support.comment: Nothing beyond standard Rust tooling (rust-analyzer just works).

rendering-backend.comment: Renders via glow: OpenGL 3.3 on desktop and WebGL2 in the browser. Not wgpu-based.

lighting.comment: No first-party lighting system; any lighting is hand-rolled via custom shaders.

custom-shaders.comment: First-class custom shaders (GLSL, cross-compiled for the GL/WebGL backends) slotted into pipelines via the graphics API.

sprites-tilemaps.comment: Built-in batched 2D sprite/image drawing via the draw2d API; no first-party tilemap system.

particle-system.comment: No built-in particle/VFX system in core.

post-processing.comment: No built-in post-processing stack; effects can be hand-rolled with render textures and custom shaders.

text-rendering.comment: Built-in text rendering (notan_text / glyph modules with font loading and a glyph atlas).

physics.comment: No physics in core; bring-your-own (e.g. rapier).

audio.comment: Audio ships with the framework (notan::audio) for loading and playing sounds; the backend is built on oddio.

input.comment: First-party keyboard and mouse input. notan_input also exposes gamepad and touch, but both are marked work-in-progress, so only keyboard/mouse is tagged as solid.

animation.comment: No first-party animation system; the repo has animation examples but they are hand-rolled (advancing frames/tweening manually).

ui-system.comment: First-party egui integration (notan::egui) is the de-facto in-game/tooling UI approach.

web-wasm.comment: First-class web target: compiles to wasm32 and runs via WebGL2; the web is treated as a first-class platform, with a hosted examples gallery.

ios.comment: Not officially supported/tested; mobile is mentioned as a goal but not a documented, shipped target.

android.comment: Not officially supported/tested; mobile is mentioned as a goal but not a documented, shipped target.

clean-build-time.comment: Not independently measured this pass. notan's dependency tree is moderate (glow/GL stack plus optional plugins) — heavier than macroquad but far lighter than Bevy — but no verified clean-build number on a stated machine was captured.

incremental-build-time.comment: Not benchmarked this pass; estimate based on the modest dependency tree (a one-line change mainly relinks a small binary). Estimate, not a measurement.

binary-size.comment: Not measured this pass; a minimal notan app produces a small stripped release binary (single-digit to low-double-digit MB). Estimate.

web-bundle-size.comment: Not measured this pass; minimal notan WASM bundles are typically a few MB after wasm-opt. Estimate.

runtime-overhead.comment: Subjective: a thin multimedia layer with no ECS/scheduler, so idle overhead is low. Not benchmarked.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time crates.io downloads of the notan crate as of 2026-07-06.

last-release.comment: Release date of the newest stable version 0.14.0.

shipped-titles.comment: No widely documented released games; notan is used mostly for demos, tools, and multimedia apps rather than notable shipped titles.

community-size.comment: Small community (~1k GitHub stars) centred on the solo lead maintainer; a handful of contributors.

plugin-asset-ecosystem.comment: A few first-party plugins/extensions (egui, text, extra) but no significant third-party plugin registry.

learning-resources.comment: A broad examples/ directory and a hosted examples gallery, plus partial docs.rs API docs (~60% documented); no official book, tutorials, or notable video ecosystem.
